profile.name: Mehebub Alom
profile.bio: An Aspiring Full Stack Developer, built various AI, Non-AI , Robotics Projects. Love to code on competative platform like CodeForces, LeetCode, etc.

prs.title: Enhance agent architecture with new features and utilities
added fix
feat: add multi-user auth with per-user data isolation
prs.body:
- Add User model to Prisma schema with email, hashed_password, store_type
- Add /api/auth/signup and /api/auth/login endpoints with bcrypt hashing
- Enforce signup before login (no account = no access)
- Each user gets a unique UUID, all data queries scoped to user_id
- New users start with completely empty data (no seeded demo data)
- Update frontend AuthContext to use real backend auth endpoints
- Update API client to send real JWT tokens from localStorage
- Auth form defaults to signup view with success message flow

Add json-user-filter: filter active users from JSON array
prs.body: ## Summary - Adds a new `json-user-filter` task in `harbor_tasks/` - Agent reads `/app/input.json` (array of user objects), filters to active users, writes `/app/output.json` - Difficulty: easy | Category: data-processing ## Task structure ---

prs.title: Add json-user-filter example task
prs.body: ## Summary - Adds a new `json-user-filter` example task demonstrating JSON data processing in the Harbor task format - Task requires an agent to filter a JSON array of users by `active` status and write the result to `output.json` - Follows the exact same reward-based architecture as the existing `hello-world` reference task ## Task structure ---

prs.title: Add json-user-filter example task with reward-based evaluation
prs.body: ## Summary - Adds a new `json-user-filter` example task that demonstrates JSON data processing in the Harbor task format - Task requires filtering a JSON array of users to include only active users and writing the result to `output.json` - Follows the exact same reward-based architecture as the `hello-world` reference task ## Commit breakdown | Commit | Purpose | |--------|---------| | Add json-user-filter task scaffolding | `task.toml`, `Dockerfile` (ubuntu:24.04 + jq), `input.json` | | Add agent instruction | `instruction.md` defining the task for agents | | Add oracle solution | `solve.sh` using `jq` to produce correct output | | Add reward-based test evaluation | `test.sh` + `test_state.py` — writes reward to `/logs/verifier/reward.txt` | ## Task structure ---

update Marathi translations and add missing keys
prs.body: This PR updates the Marathi localization file (src/i18n/locales/mr.js) to include missing translations for keys such as open_recent, no_saved_diagrams, and others that were previously defaulting to English or missing. This ensures a more complete and consistent Marathi user experience. before:- after:-
